content_en:
Looking at those two opinions, it seems best to choose the opinion of the Malikis. This is because if the fiancée is forced to return what was given of the dower, although he joined with her in buying the items or permitted her to do so, even if indirectly by not stopping her, that would cause her some harm because it will not be easy for her to sell those items at the same price that she bought them for. Furthermore, she may not need them and therefore she will face a financial penalty although she did not commit any wrong.

This is if the marriage was called off from the side of the husband. If it is called off from the fiancée’s side, the man has the definite right to demand what he had given as dower, in order to prevent him from being harmed.3

Ruling Concerning What Was Given as a Gift

There are four opinions among the jurists concerning requesting the fiancée to return what was given to her as a present.

The first opinion: It is allowed to retum the gift if the gift is still in the possession of the one to whom it is given and she had not done any kind of transaction with it that took it out of her possession. If it had been consumed or if it had been changed, such as material that was sewn, or if the woman had dispensed with it, then the man has no right to request its return or to seek something similar to it or its value.

3 Bidaayah al-Mujtabid wa Nibaayab al-Mugtasid by ibn Rushd, vol. 2, p. 21. 3 Al-Mudawwanabh al-Kubra, vol. 2, p. 177. 5 flbaar Aqd al-Zawaaj fi al-Shareeab al-Islaamiyyah by Ahmad Uthmaan, pp. 48-

‘The Figh of Marriage

Similarly, he cannot seek its return if it were perishable food items, even if it is still existing. This is the view of the Hanafis.!

The second opinion: Nothing of these gifts is to be retumed to the man, even if the marriage was called off by the woman, unless such has been made a condition or it is the prevailing custom among the people. This is the view of some of the Malikis.*

The third opinion: The man may request the return of all gifts no matter what type they were. If the gifts still exist as they were, they are to be returned. If one cannot return the actual gift, one must then return their value. This is the opinion of the majority of the Shafi’ees.3

The fourth opinion: If the proposal was ended by the party who had received the gifts, they must return the gifts. This is because the reason the presents were given was never fulfilled. If the proposal was ended by the party giving the gifts, he has no right to request the retum of the gifts. This is the opinion of al-Rafi’ee among the Shafi’ees, ibn Rushd and al-Liqaani of the Malikis and Shaikh al-Islaam ibn Taimiya.+

From the above presentation of the different views, it is clear that the most just opinion, without any doubt, is that the woman must return the gift itself, if it is still existing with her, or its value, if it has been destroyed or consumed, when the end to the engagement came from her side. This is because it is unjust for the man, the gift giver, to have to face the pain of his engagement coming to an end as well as endure a financial penalty. Similarly, if the engagement is broken off by the gift-giver, then the gifts are not returned to him so that the woman is not faced with the pain of the engagement being broken off and having to return the gifts she received.> Allah knows best.

5 Radd al-Mubtaar ala al-Durr al-Mukbtaar ‘by ibn Abideen, vol, 2, p. 374, 2 ‘4l-Furoog by al-Qaraafi, vol. a p. 148; Abkaam al-Abwaal al-Shakbsiyyab by jul Rahmaan Taaj, BP. 172-173, Hashiyataa alyooot wa Umairab ala Sharb al-Minbaaj, vol. 3, p. 216; Qawaaid qs -Abkaam, p. 56. Tebtiyaaraat ibn Taimiyab, p. 138; Bidaayab al-Mujtabid, vol. 2, pp. 21-23; al-

eer Aq al-Zawaaj fi al-Shareeab al-Islaamiyyah, p. 50.

‘The Figh of Marriage

Engagement Rings: Their Ruling for Either Men or Women

An engagement ring is often one of those gifts that the fiance gives the fiancée before the marriage contract. This is a newly adopted custom and an objectionable innovation.

The engagement is actually just an initial request and preparatory step towards the marriage and it is only by the marriage itself that the rights and obligations are established. That is the nature of the engagement or proposal. However, some people— may Allah guide them— have introduced many customs and forms into the engagement. For the proposal or engagement, they have a big party in which they invite members of the man’s family and members of the woman’s family. They prepare special foods, desserts, coffee and tea for that occasion. They have other customs and practices for that occasion as well; in some lands there are more while in others there are fewer. They do that to announce the engagement. On that occasion, some of the Muslims recite surah al-Faatiha. Also, the fiance gives the fiancée an engagement ring. The engagement becomes known to everybody by the woman wearing the engagement ring. There is nothing in Islam that indicates that such a practice should take place. In fact, it is following the customs of others, as such a practice was a Pharaonic practice while others say that it is the custom of the Christians.! In any case, the exchange of engagement rings is a foreign practice that has been adopted by Muslims and it does not form part of the religion at all.

“The jurists are agreed that it is permissible for the women to wear a gold ring and that such is forbidden for men.” The evidence that gold rings are forbidden for men and permissible for women are too many to recount. Such evidence includes what Abu Hurairah reported that the Prophet (peace be upon him) prohibited gold rings [for men].3

The Figh of Marriage

Ibn Umar stated that the Prophet (peace be upon him) used to have a ring of gold and he would wear it on his right hand. The people then followed suit and wore gold rings. Then the Prophet (peace be upon him) discarded it and said,

Ogi LY “I will never wear it.” So the people also discarded their rings."

It has been stated that Abu Bakr ibn Hazm said that such rings are permissible for men and it has also been narrated from another that such rings are simply disliked and not forbidden. In his commentary to Sahih Muslim,2 al-Nawawi wrote, “Those two reports are incorrect and those who stated them are defeated by these hadith.”

Furthermore, it is a case of blind imitation and resembling the disbelievers. The Prophet (peace be upon him) warned us about

resembling, imitating and following the disbelievers. Ibn Umar narrated that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said,

ose 230 0% ce gee ee ot Cott Aw oe “Whoever resembles a people is one of them.”3

The prohibition due to that cause, due to the act of imitation or resembling the disbelievers, includes all forms of engagement rings, whether they are of gold or of other material. Furthermore, this prohibition covers equally both men and women.

The Muslim who resembles the disbelievers, in any form of outward resemblance, such as in his clothing, manners or movements, is indicating that he has an inward feeling of love for them that is demonstrated by his resembling them. This is true because imitation comes from being pleased with them or from a feeling that the others are

' Recorded by Muslim in his Sahib (#2091), the Book of Dress, Chapter on the erie of of gold Tings for men. 3 Vol.

Vol 14 by Abu Dawood (#4031); Ahmad in a/-Musnad, vol. 2, p. 50; Shaikh alIslam ibn Taimiyah considered its chain food, as he stated in I a 1a al-Siraat alMustageem, vol. 1, p. 236 and Majmoo al-Fatawa, vol. 25, p. 331

‘The Figh of Marriage

better than the person himself. This implies ignorance of the laws of the religion. A Muslim must have [due to his being a believer in Allah]

enough self-respect that it keeps him from falling into these types of acts of imitation.

‘The Figh of Marriage

Chapter Two Announcing the Marriage and Making it Known

The Ruling Concerning Making the Marriage Known

The majority of the scholars say that making the marriage known publicly is a recommended act.

Al-Zuhri was of the opinion that the announcing of the marriage is obligatory. In fact, he said that if secret marriage is contracted and two male witnesses were present but those witnesses were ordered to keep the marriage secret, then one must separate the man and the woman. The woman is then to enter her waiting period and she is to receive her dower. If they insist on getting married before her waiting period is over, they may remarry but they must publicly announce the marriage.!

The Meaning of Announcing the Marriage

To announce the marriage is to publicize it and spread the news about it.

1 Al-Mausooab al-Fighiyyab, vol. 5, p. 262 (Wizaarah al-Auqaaf, Kuwait).

‘The Figh of Marriage

What Constitutes Announcing the Marriage

This can be done by beating the handdrum and by permissible singing. It is preferred on joyous occasions to spread joy and happiness among the people. Such occasions include the days of eid, weddings, when a person who has been gone arrives, wedding feasts, ageegahs! or at the time of having a child. Islam permits joy, happiness and pleasure within the limits of general manners and the confines of the shareeah, It is something that relaxes one soul and the soul craves such feelings according to what is natural in humans; and Islam is the religion of proper behavior and of what fits the natural tendency of humans. It is also a realistic or practical religion and it is not based on unreachable examples or dreams. Instead, it deals with humans on practical grounds and in our earthly existence. It deals with humans as beings that eat food and walk through the marketplaces. For that reason, it does not require that every word that comes from his mouth be a type of remembrance of Allah or that every moment of silence is in deep introspection or that everything he hears is only the Quran or that all of his free time is spent in the mosque. Islam recognizes his natural inclinations and needs that Allah has made in him. And Allah has created him with that natural inclination towards happiness and joyfulness in the same way that he is inclined to food and drink.
content_id:
Melihat kedua pendapat tersebut, tampaknya lebih baik memilih pendapat ulama Malikiyyah. Hal ini karena apabila tunangan wanita dipaksa mengembalikan apa yang telah diberikan dari mahar, meskipun ia telah bergabung dengannya dalam membeli barang-barang tersebut atau mengizinkannya melakukan hal itu, bahkan secara tidak langsung dengan tidak mencegahnya, hal itu akan menimbulkan kerugian baginya karena tidak mudah baginya untuk menjual barang-barang tersebut dengan harga yang sama saat ia membelinya. Selain itu, ia mungkin tidak membutuhkannya sehingga ia akan menghadapi kerugian finansial meskipun ia tidak melakukan kesalahan apa pun.

Ini berlaku apabila pernikahan dibatalkan dari pihak laki-laki. Jika dibatalkan dari pihak tunangan wanita, maka laki-laki berhak sepenuhnya untuk menuntut pengembalian apa yang telah diberikannya sebagai mahar, guna mencegah kerugian yang menimpanya.3

Hukum Mengenai Apa yang Diberikan sebagai Hadiah

Terdapat empat pendapat di kalangan para fuqaha mengenai permintaan kepada tunangan wanita untuk mengembalikan apa yang telah diberikan kepadanya sebagai hadiah.

Pendapat pertama: Diperbolehkan mengembalikan hadiah apabila hadiah tersebut masih berada dalam penguasaan penerimanya dan ia belum melakukan transaksi apa pun dengannya yang mengeluarkannya dari penguasaannya. Apabila hadiah tersebut telah habis digunakan atau telah berubah, seperti kain yang telah dijahit, atau apabila wanita tersebut telah menggunakannya, maka laki-laki tidak berhak menuntut pengembaliannya, mencari yang serupa dengannya, atau meminta nilainya.

3 Bidaayah al-Mujtahid wa Nihaayah al-Muqtasid karya Ibnu Rusyd, jil. 2, hal. 21. 3 Al-Mudawwanah al-Kubra, jil. 2, hal. 177. 5 Ithbaat Aqd al-Zawaaj fi al-Sharee'ah al-Islaamiyyah karya Ahmad Utsman, hal. 48-

Fiqh Pernikahan

Demikian pula, ia tidak dapat menuntut pengembaliannya apabila hadiah tersebut berupa makanan yang mudah rusak, meskipun masih ada. Ini adalah pendapat ulama Hanafiyyah.!

Pendapat kedua: Tidak ada satu pun dari hadiah-hadiah tersebut yang dikembalikan kepada laki-laki, bahkan apabila pernikahan dibatalkan oleh pihak wanita, kecuali apabila hal itu telah dijadikan syarat atau merupakan adat kebiasaan yang berlaku di kalangan masyarakat. Ini adalah pendapat sebagian ulama Malikiyyah.*

Pendapat ketiga: Laki-laki boleh menuntut pengembalian semua hadiah apa pun jenisnya. Apabila hadiah-hadiah tersebut masih ada sebagaimana adanya, maka dikembalikan. Apabila hadiah aslinya tidak dapat dikembalikan, maka nilainya wajib dikembalikan. Ini adalah pendapat mayoritas ulama Syafi'iyyah.3

Pendapat keempat: Apabila lamaran diakhiri oleh pihak yang menerima hadiah, mereka wajib mengembalikan hadiah-hadiah tersebut. Hal ini karena alasan diberikannya hadiah-hadiah tersebut tidak pernah terpenuhi. Apabila lamaran diakhiri oleh pihak yang memberikan hadiah, maka ia tidak berhak menuntut pengembalian hadiah-hadiah tersebut. Ini adalah pendapat al-Rafi'i di kalangan ulama Syafi'iyyah, Ibnu Rusyd dan al-Liqqani di kalangan ulama Malikiyyah, serta Syaikhul Islam Ibnu Taimiyyah.+

Dari paparan berbagai pendapat di atas, jelaslah bahwa pendapat yang paling adil, tanpa keraguan sedikit pun, adalah bahwa wanita tersebut wajib mengembalikan hadiah itu sendiri, apabila masih ada padanya, atau nilainya, apabila telah rusak atau habis digunakan, ketika berakhirnya pertunangan berasal dari pihaknya. Hal ini karena tidak adil bagi laki-laki, sang pemberi hadiah, untuk harus merasakan kepedihan berakhirnya pertunangan sekaligus menanggung kerugian finansial. Demikian pula, apabila pertunangan diputus oleh pihak pemberi hadiah, maka hadiah-hadiah tersebut tidak dikembalikan kepadanya agar wanita tersebut tidak harus menghadapi kepedihan putusnya pertunangan sekaligus harus mengembalikan hadiah-hadiah yang telah diterimanya.> Allah Maha Mengetahui.

5 Radd al-Muhtaar 'ala al-Durr al-Mukhtaar karya Ibnu Abidin, jil. 2, hal. 374. 2 Al-Furuuq karya al-Qaraafi, jil. 4, hal. 148; Ahkaam al-Ahwaal al-Shakhsiyyah karya 'Abd al-Rahman Taaj, hal. 172-173; Haashiyataa al-Qalyuubi wa 'Umairah 'ala Sharh al-Minhaaj, jil. 3, hal. 216; Qawaaid al-Ahkaam, hal. 56; Ikhtiyaaraat Ibnu Taimiyyah, hal. 138; Bidaayah al-Mujtahid, jil. 2, hal. 21-23; al-

Ithbaat Aqd al-Zawaaj fi al-Sharee'ah al-Islaamiyyah, hal. 50.

Fiqh Pernikahan

Cincin Pertunangan: Hukumnya bagi Laki-Laki maupun Perempuan

Cincin pertunangan seringkali merupakan salah satu hadiah yang diberikan oleh sang laki-laki kepada tunangan wanitanya sebelum akad nikah. Ini adalah adat yang baru diadopsi dan merupakan bid'ah yang tercela.

Pertunangan sebenarnya hanyalah permintaan awal dan langkah persiapan menuju pernikahan, dan hanya dengan pernikahan itulah hak dan kewajiban ditetapkan. Itulah hakikat pertunangan atau lamaran. Namun, sebagian orang — semoga Allah memberi mereka hidayah — telah memperkenalkan banyak adat dan bentuk dalam pertunangan. Untuk lamaran atau pertunangan, mereka mengadakan pesta besar di mana mereka mengundang anggota keluarga laki-laki dan anggota keluarga perempuan. Mereka menyiapkan makanan khusus, kue, kopi, dan teh untuk acara tersebut. Mereka juga memiliki adat dan praktik lain untuk kesempatan itu; di beberapa daerah lebih banyak sementara di daerah lain lebih sedikit. Mereka melakukan itu untuk mengumumkan pertunangan. Pada kesempatan itu, sebagian kaum muslimin membaca surah al-Fatihah. Selain itu, sang laki-laki memberikan cincin pertunangan kepada tunangan wanitanya. Pertunangan menjadi diketahui semua orang melalui wanita yang memakai cincin pertunangan. Tidak ada dalam Islam yang menunjukkan bahwa praktik semacam itu seharusnya terjadi. Bahkan, hal itu adalah mengikuti adat orang lain, karena praktik seperti itu merupakan adat Fir'auniyyah sementara yang lain mengatakan bahwa itu adalah adat orang-orang Kristen.! Bagaimanapun juga, saling bertukar cincin pertunangan adalah praktik asing yang telah diadopsi oleh kaum muslimin dan sama sekali tidak termasuk bagian dari agama.

"Para fuqaha sepakat bahwa diperbolehkan bagi wanita untuk memakai cincin emas dan bahwa hal itu diharamkan bagi laki-laki." Dalil-dalil bahwa cincin emas diharamkan bagi laki-laki dan diperbolehkan bagi wanita sangat banyak untuk disebutkan. Di antara dalil tersebut adalah apa yang diriwayatkan oleh Abu Hurairah bahwa Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) melarang cincin emas [bagi laki-laki].3

Fiqh Pernikahan

Ibnu Umar menyatakan bahwa Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) dahulu memakai cincin emas dan beliau memakainya di tangan kanannya. Orang-orang pun mengikuti beliau dan memakai cincin emas. Kemudian Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) melepaskannya dan bersabda,

Ogi LY "Aku tidak akan pernah memakainya lagi." Maka orang-orang pun melepaskan cincin mereka."

Disebutkan bahwa Abu Bakr ibn Hazm mengatakan bahwa cincin-cincin tersebut diperbolehkan bagi laki-laki, dan juga diriwayatkan dari orang lain bahwa cincin-cincin tersebut hanya dimakruhkan dan tidak diharamkan. Dalam syarahnya terhadap Shahih Muslim,2 al-Nawawi menulis, "Kedua riwayat tersebut tidak benar dan orang-orang yang menyatakannya telah dikalahkan oleh hadith-hadith ini."

Selain itu, hal itu merupakan taklid buta dan menyerupai orang-orang kafir. Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) telah memperingatkan kita tentang

menyerupai, meniru, dan mengikuti orang-orang kafir. Ibnu Umar meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda,

ose 230 0% ce gee ee ot Cott Aw oe "Barangsiapa menyerupai suatu kaum maka ia termasuk bagian dari mereka."3

Larangan karena sebab tersebut, yaitu karena perbuatan meniru atau menyerupai orang-orang kafir, mencakup semua bentuk cincin pertunangan, baik terbuat dari emas maupun bahan lainnya. Selain itu, larangan ini berlaku sama bagi laki-laki maupun perempuan.

Seorang muslim yang menyerupai orang-orang kafir, dalam bentuk apa pun dari penyerupaan lahiriah, seperti dalam pakaian, perilaku, atau gerak-geriknya, sedang menunjukkan bahwa ia memiliki rasa cinta batiniah kepada mereka yang dibuktikan dengan penyerupaannya kepada mereka. Hal ini benar karena peniruan berasal dari rasa puas terhadap mereka atau dari perasaan bahwa orang lain lebih

' Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Shahih-nya (#2091), Kitab Pakaian, Bab Haramnya cincin emas bagi laki-laki. 3 Jil.

Jil. 14 oleh Abu Dawood (#4031); Ahmad dalam al-Musnad, jil. 2, hal. 50; Syaikhul Islam Ibnu Taimiyyah menilai sanadnya baik, sebagaimana yang beliau nyatakan dalam Iqtidaa' al-Siraat al-Mustaqeem, jil. 1, hal. 236 dan Majmuu' al-Fatawa, jil. 25, hal. 331

Fiqh Pernikahan

baik dari orang itu sendiri. Hal ini mengisyaratkan ketidaktahuan terhadap hukum-hukum agama. Seorang muslim harus memiliki [karena keimanannya kepada Allah]

harga diri yang cukup sehingga mencegahnya dari terjerumus ke dalam bentuk-bentuk peniruan semacam ini.

Fiqh Pernikahan

Bab Dua Mengumumkan Pernikahan dan Menjadikannya Diketahui Umum

Hukum Menjadikan Pernikahan Diketahui Umum

Mayoritas ulama mengatakan bahwa menjadikan pernikahan diketahui secara publik adalah perbuatan yang dianjurkan.

Al-Zuhri berpendapat bahwa pengumuman pernikahan adalah wajib. Bahkan, beliau mengatakan bahwa apabila pernikahan rahasia dilangsungkan dan dua orang saksi laki-laki hadir namun para saksi tersebut diperintahkan untuk merahasiakan pernikahan tersebut, maka suami istri tersebut wajib dipisahkan. Wanita itu kemudian menjalani masa iddahnya dan ia berhak mendapatkan maharnya. Apabila mereka bersikeras untuk menikah sebelum masa iddahnya berakhir, mereka boleh menikah kembali namun mereka wajib mengumumkan pernikahan tersebut secara publik.!

Makna Mengumumkan Pernikahan

Mengumumkan pernikahan berarti mempublikasikannya dan menyebarkan berita tentangnya.

1 Al-Mausoo'ah al-Fiqhiyyah, jil. 5, hlm. 262 (Wizaarah al-Auqaaf, Kuwait).

Fiqh Pernikahan

Hal-hal yang Termasuk dalam Pengumuman Pernikahan

Hal ini dapat dilakukan dengan memukul rebana dan dengan nyanyian yang dibolehkan. Hal ini dianjurkan pada kesempatan-kesempatan yang menggembirakan untuk menyebarkan kegembiraan dan kebahagiaan di antara manusia. Kesempatan-kesempatan tersebut mencakup hari-hari eid, pernikahan, ketika seseorang yang telah lama pergi tiba kembali, walimah, aqiqah, atau pada saat kelahiran anak. Islam mengizinkan kegembiraan, kebahagiaan, dan kesenangan dalam batas-batas adab yang umum dan dalam koridor syariah. Hal itu merupakan sesuatu yang menenangkan jiwa, dan jiwa memang mendambakan perasaan-perasaan seperti itu sesuai dengan fitrah yang ada pada manusia; dan Islam adalah agama perilaku yang baik dan yang sesuai dengan kecenderungan fitrah manusia. Islam juga merupakan agama yang realistis atau praktis, dan ia tidak didasarkan pada contoh-contoh yang tidak terjangkau atau angan-angan belaka. Sebaliknya, Islam berhubungan dengan manusia di atas dasar yang praktis dan dalam kehidupan dunia kita. Islam berhubungan dengan manusia sebagai makhluk yang makan makanan dan berjalan di pasar-pasar. Oleh karena itu, Islam tidak mensyaratkan bahwa setiap kata yang keluar dari mulutnya haruslah berupa dzikir kepada Allah, atau bahwa setiap saat diamnya berada dalam perenungan yang mendalam, atau bahwa setiap yang didengarnya hanyalah Al-Quran, atau bahwa seluruh waktu luangnya dihabiskan di masjid. Islam mengakui kecenderungan dan kebutuhan alaminya yang telah Allah ciptakan dalam dirinya. Dan Allah telah menciptakannya dengan kecenderungan fitrah menuju kebahagiaan dan kegembiraan, sebagaimana ia juga cenderung kepada makanan dan minuman.
content_ar:
Looking at those two opinions, it seems best to choose the opinion of the Malikis. This is because if the fiancée is forced to return what was given of the dower, although he joined with her in buying the items or permitted her to do so, even if indirectly by not stopping her, that would cause her some harm because it will not be easy for her to sell those items at the same price that she bought them for. Furthermore, she may not need them and therefore she will face a financial penalty although she did not commit any wrong.

This is if the marriage was called off from the side of the husband. If it is called off from the fiancée’s side, the man has the definite right to demand what he had given as dower, in order to prevent him from being harmed.3

Ruling Concerning What Was Given as a Gift

There are four opinions among the jurists concerning requesting the fiancée to return what was given to her as a present.

The first opinion: It is allowed to retum the gift if the gift is still in the possession of the one to whom it is given and she had not done any kind of transaction with it that took it out of her possession. If it had been consumed or if it had been changed, such as material that was sewn, or if the woman had dispensed with it, then the man has no right to request its return or to seek something similar to it or its value.

3 Bidaayah al-Mujtabid wa Nibaayab al-Mugtasid by ibn Rushd, vol. 2, p. 21. 3 Al-Mudawwanabh al-Kubra, vol. 2, p. 177. 5 flbaar Aqd al-Zawaaj fi al-Shareeab al-Islaamiyyah by Ahmad Uthmaan, pp. 48-

‘The Figh of Marriage

Similarly, he cannot seek its return if it were perishable food items, even if it is still existing. This is the view of the Hanafis.!

The second opinion: Nothing of these gifts is to be retumed to the man, even if the marriage was called off by the woman, unless such has been made a condition or it is the prevailing custom among the people. This is the view of some of the Malikis.*

The third opinion: The man may request the return of all gifts no matter what type they were. If the gifts still exist as they were, they are to be returned. If one cannot return the actual gift, one must then return their value. This is the opinion of the majority of the Shafi’ees.3

The fourth opinion: If the proposal was ended by the party who had received the gifts, they must return the gifts. This is because the reason the presents were given was never fulfilled. If the proposal was ended by the party giving the gifts, he has no right to request the retum of the gifts. This is the opinion of al-Rafi’ee among the Shafi’ees, ibn Rushd and al-Liqaani of the Malikis and Shaikh al-Islaam ibn Taimiya.+

From the above presentation of the different views, it is clear that the most just opinion, without any doubt, is that the woman must return the gift itself, if it is still existing with her, or its value, if it has been destroyed or consumed, when the end to the engagement came from her side. This is because it is unjust for the man, the gift giver, to have to face the pain of his engagement coming to an end as well as endure a financial penalty. Similarly, if the engagement is broken off by the gift-giver, then the gifts are not returned to him so that the woman is not faced with the pain of the engagement being broken off and having to return the gifts she received.> الله knows best.

eer Aq al-Zawaaj fi al-Shareeab al-Islaamiyyah, p. 50.

‘The Figh of Marriage

Engagement Rings: Their Ruling for Either Men or Women

An engagement ring is often one of those gifts that the fiance gives the fiancée before the marriage contract. This is a newly adopted custom and an objectionable innovation.

The engagement is actually just an initial request and preparatory step towards the marriage and it is only by the marriage itself that the rights and obligations are established. That is the nature of the engagement or proposal. However, some people— may الله guide them— have introduced many customs and forms into the engagement. For the proposal or engagement, they have a big party in which they invite members of the man’s family and members of the woman’s family. They prepare special foods, desserts, coffee and tea for that occasion. They have other customs and practices for that occasion as well; in some lands there are more while in others there are fewer. They do that to announce the engagement. On that occasion, some of the Muslims recite surah al-Faatiha. Also, the fiance gives the fiancée an engagement ring. The engagement becomes known to everybody by the woman wearing the engagement ring. There is nothing in Islam that indicates that such a practice should take place. In fact, it is following the customs of others, as such a practice was a Pharaonic practice while others say that it is the custom of the Christians.! In any case, the exchange of engagement rings is a foreign practice that has been adopted by Muslims and it does not form part of the religion at all.

“The jurists are agreed that it is permissible for the women to wear a gold ring and that such is forbidden for men.” The evidence that gold rings are forbidden for men and permissible for women are too many to recount. Such evidence includes what Abu Hurairah reported that النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) prohibited gold rings [for men].3

The Figh of Marriage

Ibn Umar stated that النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) used to have a ring of gold and he would wear it on his right hand. The people then followed suit and wore gold rings. Then النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) discarded it and said,

Ogi LY “I will never wear it.” So the people also discarded their rings."

It has been stated that Abu Bakr ibn Hazm said that such rings are permissible for men and it has also been narrated from another that such rings are simply disliked and not forbidden. In his commentary to Sahih Muslim,2 al-Nawawi wrote, “Those two reports are incorrect and those who stated them are defeated by these الحديث.”

Furthermore, it is a case of blind imitation and resembling the disbelievers. النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) warned us about

resembling, imitating and following the disbelievers. Ibn Umar narrated that رسول الله ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) said,

ose 230 0% ce gee ee ot Cott Aw oe “Whoever resembles a people is one of them.”3

The prohibition due to that cause, due to the act of imitation or resembling the disbelievers, includes all forms of engagement rings, whether they are of gold or of other material. Furthermore, this prohibition covers equally both men and women.

The Muslim who resembles the disbelievers, in any form of outward resemblance, such as in his clothing, manners or movements, is indicating that he has an inward feeling of love for them that is demonstrated by his resembling them. This is true because imitation comes from being pleased with them or from a feeling that the others are

‘The Figh of Marriage

better than the person himself. This implies ignorance of the laws of the religion. A Muslim must have [due to his being a believer in الله]

enough self-respect that it keeps him from falling into these types of acts of imitation.

‘The Figh of Marriage

Chapter Two Announcing the Marriage and Making it Known

The Ruling Concerning Making the Marriage Known

The majority of the scholars say that making the marriage known publicly is a recommended act.

Al-Zuhri was of the opinion that the announcing of the marriage is obligatory. In fact, he said that if secret marriage is contracted and two male witnesses were present but those witnesses were ordered to keep the marriage secret, then one must separate the man and the woman. The woman is then to enter her waiting period and she is to receive her dower. If they insist on getting married before her waiting period is over, they may remarry but they must publicly announce the marriage.!

The Meaning of Announcing the Marriage

To announce the marriage is to publicize it and spread the news about it.

1 Al-Mausooab al-Fighiyyab, vol. 5, p. 262 (Wizaarah al-Auqaaf, Kuwait).

‘The Figh of Marriage

What Constitutes Announcing the Marriage

This can be done by beating the handdrum and by permissible singing. It is preferred on joyous occasions to spread joy and happiness among the people. Such occasions include the days of eid, weddings, when a person who has been gone arrives, wedding feasts, ageegahs! or at the time of having a child. Islam permits joy, happiness and pleasure within the limits of general manners and the confines of the shareeah, It is something that relaxes one soul and the soul craves such feelings according to what is natural in humans; and Islam is the religion of proper behavior and of what fits the natural tendency of humans. It is also a realistic or practical religion and it is not based on unreachable examples or dreams. Instead, it deals with humans on practical grounds and in our earthly existence. It deals with humans as beings that eat food and walk through the marketplaces. For that reason, it does not require that every word that comes from his mouth be a type of remembrance of الله or that every moment of silence is in deep introspection or that everything he hears is only the القرآن الكريم or that all of his free time is spent in the mosque. Islam recognizes his natural inclinations and needs that الله has made in him. And الله has created him with that natural inclination towards happiness and joyfulness in the same way that he is inclined to food and drink.
